revision.text:
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] {{Lunisolar May}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May|12=draft|12=draft}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Bunkyū|ja|文久|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1862]] {{ill|June 26|ja|6月26日|12=draft}}) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=draft}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4}}</ref> * [[Meiji 4]] ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1872]] {{ill|July 16|ja|7月16日|12=draft}}) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1872]] {{ill|July 4|ja|7月4日|12=draft}}) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[Ōei]] 11th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[1404]] {{ill|July 6|ja|7月6日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|ja|山名宗全|12=draft}} (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2}}</ref>. * [[Eishō]] 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[1517]] {{ill|June 18|ja|6月18日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Shin-chikamachi Tennō|ja|正親町天皇|12=draft}}, the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[1593]]). * [[Genna]] 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1618]] {{ill|July 21|ja|7月21日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Ga-hō|ja|林鵞峰|12=draft}} (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[1680]]). * {{ill|Bunsei|ja|文政|12=draft|12=draft}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1819]] {{ill|July 20|ja|7月20日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Saion|ja|徳川斉温|12=draft}}, the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=draft}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[1919]]).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久|12=draft|12=draft}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} [[1193]] {{ill|June 29|ja|6月29日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=draft}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[1174]]). * [[Tenmei]] 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1788]] {{ill|July 2|ja|7月2日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=draft}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[Bunka]] 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} [[1817]] {{ill|July 13|ja|7月13日|12=draft}}) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=draft}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[1755]]).
== Commemorations == <!-- Add commemorations if needed -->
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529}}
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] [[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]]
==References== {{reflist}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunisolar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar May]] [[Category:Senbu]] [[Category:Days 29 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Lunisolar]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:May 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 5월 29일]] [[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:五月廿九]] [[en:draft:May 29 (Lunisolar)]] [[sn:May 29日 (旧暦)]] [[sn:ay 29 (Lunisolar)]]

revision.text:
{{旧暦5月}} '''旧暦5月29日'''は、[[5月 (旧暦)|旧暦5月]]の29日目である。年によっては5月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#先負|先負]]である。
== できごと == * [[文久]]2年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1862年]][[6月26日]]） - [[東禅寺事件#第二次東禅寺事件|第二次東禅寺事件]]{{要出典|date=2021-03}}。江戸・東禅寺のイギリス仮公使館に仮公使館を警備していた松本藩士が侵入しイギリス人水兵2人を殺害 * [[明治4年]]（グレゴリオ暦[[1872年]][[7月16日]]） - 吉原で大火。廓内2/3が焼失。678戸が類焼<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721|isbn=4-404-03190-4 }}</ref>。 * 明治5年（グレゴリオ暦[[1872年]][[7月4日]]） - [[東京高等師範学校|東京師範学校]]（[[東京教育大学]]を経た、現在の[[筑波大学]]）設置
== 誕生日 == * [[応永]]11年（[[ユリウス暦]][[1404年]][[7月6日]]） - [[山名宗全]]（持豊）、[[武将]]（+ [[1473年]]<ref>『東寺執行日記』には「死去は18日なり。16日に大事にて、18日治定か」とある。川岡、P144 - P145。</ref>） * [[永正]]14年（ユリウス暦[[1517年]][[6月18日]]） - [[正親町天皇]]、106代[[天皇]]（+ [[1593年]]） * [[元和 (日本)|元和]]4年（グレゴリオ暦[[1618年]][[7月21日]]） - [[林鵞峰]]（春斎）、[[朱子学者]]・[[林羅山]]の子（+ [[1680年]]） * [[文政]]2年（グレゴリオ暦[[1819年]][[7月20日]]）- [[徳川斉温]]、[[尾張藩]]11代藩主（+ [[1839年]]） * 文久2年（グレゴリオ暦1862年6月26日） - [[箕作元八]]、[[歴史家]]、[[箕作秋坪]]の四男（+ [[1919年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[建久]]4年（ユリウス暦[[1193年]][[6月29日]]） - [[曾我時致|曾我五郎時致]]、[[武士]]・曾我兄弟の弟（* [[1174年]]） * [[天明]]8年（グレゴリオ暦[[1788年]][[7月2日]]） - [[呉陵軒可有]]、 雑俳作者『柳多留』 * [[文化 (元号)|文化]]14年（グレゴリオ暦[[1817年]][[7月13日]]） - [[海保青陵]]、[[儒学者]]･[[経世家]]（* [[1755年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529}}
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[5月29日 (旧暦)]] to [[May 29 (Lunisolar)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
{{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|qq=}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|qq=}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]]

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|qq=}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|qq=}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜}}.

== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).

Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|qq=|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|qq=|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

{{translated page|ja|5月29日 (旧暦)|version=103703365|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|qq=|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|qq=|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).

Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|ja|先負}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|qq=|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|qq=|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]]
{{translated page|ja|5月29日 (旧暦)|version=103703365|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|qid=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|qq=|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|qq=|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]]
{{translated page|ja|5月29日 (旧暦)|version=103703365|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar May]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]][[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Senbu]] [[Category:Days 29 of Japanese Lunar months]] }}
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:May 29 (Lunisolar)]]
[[ko:음력 5월 29일]] [[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:五月廿九]]
[[ar:May 29 (Lunisolar)]]
<!-- Add commemorations if needed -->
[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] {{wikidata link|Q839553}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Non matching pagename on wikidata]]

[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] {{wikidata link|Q839553}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "zh_min_nan" to "zh min nan"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh min nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|qid=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|qq=|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|qq=|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]]
{{translated page|ja|5月29日 (旧暦)|version=103703365|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] {{wikidata link|Q839553}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|qq=|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|qq=|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|qq=|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

{{translated page|ja|5月29日 (旧暦)|version=103703365|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] {{wikidata link|Q839553}} [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]]
{{translated page|ja|5月29日 (旧暦)|version=103703365|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:May 29 (Lunisolar)]]
[[ko:음력 5월 29일]] [[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:五月廿九]]

[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] {{wikidata link|Q839553}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
