[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] {{wikidata link|Q839553}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}

== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh_min_nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh_min_nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}[[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh_min_nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh_min_nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]]
{{translated page|ja|5月29日 (旧暦)|version=103703365|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories|
}}
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:May 29 (Lunisolar)]]
[[ko:음력 5월 29일]] [[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:五月廿九]]
[[ar:May 29 (Lunisolar)]]
<!-- Add commemorations if needed -->
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] {{wikidata link|Q839553}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Days 29 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar May]] [[Category:Senbu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]]

== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}

{{translated page|ja|5月29日 (旧暦)|version=103703365|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh_min_nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh_min_nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh_yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).

revision.comment: Text replacement - "zh_yue" to "zh-yue"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q839553]] Wikidata name: 29th day of the 5th month in the Chinese calendar [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?--> {{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * [[:en:Bunkyū|Bunkyū]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1862|1862]] [[:en:June 26|June 26]]) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 16|July 16]]) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1872|1872]] [[:en:July 4|July 4]]) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * [[:en:Ōei|Ōei]] 11th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1404|1404]] [[:en:July 6|July 6]]) – [[:en:Yamana Sōzen|Yamana Sōzen]] (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died [[:en:1473|1473]])<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 14th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1517|1517]] [[:en:June 18|June 18]]) – [[:en:Emperor Ōgimachi|Shin-chikamachi Tennō]], the 106th Emperor of Japan (died [[:en:1593|1593]]). * [[:en:Genna|Genna]] 4th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1618|1618]] [[:en:July 21|July 21]]) – [[:en:Hayashi Gahō|Hayashi Ga-hō]] (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died [[:en:1680|1680]]). * [[:en:Bunsei|Bunsei]] 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1819|1819]] [[:en:July 20|July 20]]) – [[:en:Tokugawa Nariharu|Tokugawa Saion]], the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died [[:en:1839|1839]]). * Bunkyū 2nd year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died [[:en:1919|1919]]).
== Deaths == * [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] 4th year ([[:en:Julian calendar|Julian calendar]] [[:en:1193|1193]] [[:en:June 29|June 29]]) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born [[:en:1174|1174]]). * [[:en:Tenmei|Tenmei]] 8th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1788|1788]] [[:en:July 2|July 2]]) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * [[:en:Bunka|Bunka]] 14th year ([[:en:Gregorian calendar|Gregorian calendar]] [[:en:1817|1817]] [[:en:July 13|July 13]]) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born [[:en:1755|1755]]).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}
==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
[[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]]
{{translated page|ja|5月29日 (旧暦)|version=103703365|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[de:May 29 (Lunisolar)]]
[[ko:음력 5월 29일]] [[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:五月廿九]]
[[ar:May 29 (Lunisolar)]]
<!-- Add commemorations if needed --> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:五月廿九]] {{wikidata link|Q839553}} [[Category:Completion tier 7]] [[Category:Days 29 of Japanese Lunar months]] [[Category:Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] [[Category:Lunisolar May]] [[Category:Senbu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]]

Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:16, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26}}) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}}) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 4|en|July 4|WD=Q2698|lt=July 4}}) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 11th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1404|en|1404|WD=Q6121|lt=1404}} {{ill|July 6|en|July 6|WD=Q2695|lt=July 6}}) – {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|en|Yamana Sōzen|WD=Q1355689|lt=Yamana Sōzen}} (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died {{ill|1473|en|1473|WD=Q6594|lt=1473}})<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō}} 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1517|en|1517|WD=Q6263|lt=1517}} {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|WD=Q2670|lt=June 18}}) – {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|en|Emperor Ōgimachi|WD=Q334076|lt=Shin-chikamachi Tennō}}, the 106th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1593|en|1593|WD=Q6772|lt=1593}}). * {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1618|en|1618|WD=Q6801|lt=1618}} {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|en|Hayashi Gahō|WD=Q3128811|lt=Hayashi Ga-hō}} (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died {{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} {{ill|July 20|en|July 20|WD=Q2728|lt=July 20}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Nariharu|en|Tokugawa Nariharu|WD=Q3336095|lt=Tokugawa Saion}}, the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}}). * Bunkyū 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1193|en|1193|WD=Q19741|lt=1193}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}}). * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788}} {{ill|July 2|en|July 2|WD=Q2697|lt=July 2}}) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1817|en|1817|WD=Q6958|lt=1817}} {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13}}) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755}}).

Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26}}) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}}) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 4|en|July 4|WD=Q2698|lt=July 4}}) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 11th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1404|en|1404|WD=Q6121|lt=1404}} {{ill|July 6|en|July 6|WD=Q2695|lt=July 6}}) – {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|en|Yamana Sōzen|WD=Q1355689|lt=Yamana Sōzen}} (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died {{ill|1473|en|1473|WD=Q6594|lt=1473}})<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō}} 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1517|en|1517|WD=Q6263|lt=1517}} {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|WD=Q2670|lt=June 18}}) – {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|en|Emperor Ōgimachi|WD=Q334076|lt=Shin-chikamachi Tennō}}, the 106th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1593|en|1593|WD=Q6772|lt=1593}}). * {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1618|en|1618|WD=Q6801|lt=1618}} {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|en|Hayashi Gahō|WD=Q3128811|lt=Hayashi Ga-hō}} (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died {{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} {{ill|July 20|en|July 20|WD=Q2728|lt=July 20}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Nariharu|en|Tokugawa Nariharu|WD=Q3336095|lt=Tokugawa Saion}}, the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}}). * Bunkyū 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1193|en|1193|WD=Q19741|lt=1193}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}}). * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788}} {{ill|July 2|en|July 2|WD=Q2697|lt=July 2}}) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1817|en|1817|WD=Q6958|lt=1817}} {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13}}) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755}}).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]]
{{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26}}) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}}) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 4|en|July 4|WD=Q2698|lt=July 4}}) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 11th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1404|en|1404|WD=Q6121|lt=1404}} {{ill|July 6|en|July 6|WD=Q2695|lt=July 6}}) – {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|en|Yamana Sōzen|WD=Q1355689|lt=Yamana Sōzen}} (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died {{ill|1473|en|1473|WD=Q6594|lt=1473}})<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō}} 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1517|en|1517|WD=Q6263|lt=1517}} {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|WD=Q2670|lt=June 18}}) – {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|en|Emperor Ōgimachi|WD=Q334076|lt=Shin-chikamachi Tennō}}, the 106th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1593|en|1593|WD=Q6772|lt=1593}}). * {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1618|en|1618|WD=Q6801|lt=1618}} {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|en|Hayashi Gahō|WD=Q3128811|lt=Hayashi Ga-hō}} (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died {{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} {{ill|July 20|en|July 20|WD=Q2728|lt=July 20}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Nariharu|en|Tokugawa Nariharu|WD=Q3336095|lt=Tokugawa Saion}}, the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}}). * Bunkyū 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1193|en|1193|WD=Q19741|lt=1193}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}}). * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788}} {{ill|July 2|en|July 2|WD=Q2697|lt=July 2}}) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1817|en|1817|WD=Q6958|lt=1817}} {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13}}) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755}}).

Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26}}) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}}) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 4|en|July 4|WD=Q2698|lt=July 4}}) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 11th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1404|en|1404|WD=Q6121|lt=1404}} {{ill|July 6|en|July 6|WD=Q2695|lt=July 6}}) – {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|en|Yamana Sōzen|WD=Q1355689|lt=Yamana Sōzen}} (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died {{ill|1473|en|1473|WD=Q6594|lt=1473}})<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō}} 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1517|en|1517|WD=Q6263|lt=1517}} {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|WD=Q2670|lt=June 18}}) – {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|en|Emperor Ōgimachi|WD=Q334076|lt=Shin-chikamachi Tennō}}, the 106th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1593|en|1593|WD=Q6772|lt=1593}}). * {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1618|en|1618|WD=Q6801|lt=1618}} {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|en|Hayashi Gahō|WD=Q3128811|lt=Hayashi Ga-hō}} (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died {{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} {{ill|July 20|en|July 20|WD=Q2728|lt=July 20}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Nariharu|en|Tokugawa Nariharu|WD=Q3336095|lt=Tokugawa Saion}}, the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}}). * Bunkyū 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1193|en|1193|WD=Q19741|lt=1193}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}}). * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788}} {{ill|July 2|en|July 2|WD=Q2697|lt=July 2}}) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1817|en|1817|WD=Q6958|lt=1817}} {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13}}) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755}}).
== Commemorations == == See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|5|28|5|30|4|29|6|5|0529|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:5 nguŏk 29 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]][[ja:5月29日 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 5월 29일]][[zh-min-nan:5 goe̍h 29 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]][[zh-yue:五月廿九]][[zh:五月廿九]]
{{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26}}) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}}) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 4|en|July 4|WD=Q2698|lt=July 4}}) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 11th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1404|en|1404|WD=Q6121|lt=1404}} {{ill|July 6|en|July 6|WD=Q2695|lt=July 6}}) – {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|en|Yamana Sōzen|WD=Q1355689|lt=Yamana Sōzen}} (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died {{ill|1473|en|1473|WD=Q6594|lt=1473}})<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō}} 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1517|en|1517|WD=Q6263|lt=1517}} {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|WD=Q2670|lt=June 18}}) – {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|en|Emperor Ōgimachi|WD=Q334076|lt=Shin-chikamachi Tennō}}, the 106th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1593|en|1593|WD=Q6772|lt=1593}}). * {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1618|en|1618|WD=Q6801|lt=1618}} {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|en|Hayashi Gahō|WD=Q3128811|lt=Hayashi Ga-hō}} (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died {{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} {{ill|July 20|en|July 20|WD=Q2728|lt=July 20}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Nariharu|en|Tokugawa Nariharu|WD=Q3336095|lt=Tokugawa Saion}}, the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}}). * Bunkyū 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1193|en|1193|WD=Q19741|lt=1193}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}}). * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788}} {{ill|July 2|en|July 2|WD=Q2697|lt=July 2}}) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1817|en|1817|WD=Q6958|lt=1817}} {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13}}) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755}}).

Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26}}) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}}) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} {{ill|July 4|en|July 4|WD=Q2698|lt=July 4}}) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.
== Births == * {{ill|Ōei|en|Ōei|WD=Q1193078|lt=Ōei}} 11th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1404|en|1404|WD=Q6121|lt=1404}} {{ill|July 6|en|July 6|WD=Q2695|lt=July 6}}) – {{ill|Yamana Sōzen|en|Yamana Sōzen|WD=Q1355689|lt=Yamana Sōzen}} (also known as Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died {{ill|1473|en|1473|WD=Q6594|lt=1473}})<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō}} 14th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1517|en|1517|WD=Q6263|lt=1517}} {{ill|June 18|en|June 18|WD=Q2670|lt=June 18}}) – {{ill|Emperor Ōgimachi|en|Emperor Ōgimachi|WD=Q334076|lt=Shin-chikamachi Tennō}}, the 106th Emperor of Japan (died {{ill|1593|en|1593|WD=Q6772|lt=1593}}). * {{ill|Genna|en|Genna|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna}} 4th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1618|en|1618|WD=Q6801|lt=1618}} {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|WD=Q2727|lt=July 21}}) – {{ill|Hayashi Gahō|en|Hayashi Gahō|WD=Q3128811|lt=Hayashi Ga-hō}} (also known as Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died {{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819}} {{ill|July 20|en|July 20|WD=Q2728|lt=July 20}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Nariharu|en|Tokugawa Nariharu|WD=Q3336095|lt=Tokugawa Saion}}, the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died {{ill|1839|en|1839|WD=Q7616|lt=1839}}). * Bunkyū 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1193|en|1193|WD=Q19741|lt=1193}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29}}) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174}}). * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788}} {{ill|July 2|en|July 2|WD=Q2697|lt=July 2}}) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1817|en|1817|WD=Q6958|lt=1817}} {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13}}) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755}}).

{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.

Mochiyutaka), a military commander (died {{ill|1473|en|1473|WD=Q6594|lt=1473|1=1473}})<ref>{{Harvnb|百目木|1918|p=2|qq=}}</ref>. * {{ill|Eishō|en|Eishō|WD=Q1247883|lt=Eishō|1=Eishō}} 14th year Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died {{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680|1=1680}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1819|en|1819|WD=Q6967|lt=1819|1=1819}} {{ill|July 20|en|July 20|WD=Q2728|lt=July 20|1=July 20}}) – year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū|1=Kenkyū}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|WD=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1193|en|1193|WD=Q19741|lt=1193|1=1193}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|WD=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born {{ill|1174|en|1174|WD=Q19690|lt=1174|1=1174}}). * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|WD=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|WD=Q7765|lt=1788|1=1788}} {{ill|July 2|en|July 2|WD=Q2697|lt=July 2|1=July 2}}) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|WD=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1817|en|1817|WD=Q6958|lt=1817|1=1817}} {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|WD=Q2687|lt=July 13|1=July 13}}) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born {{ill|1755|en|1755|WD=Q7627|lt=1755|1=1755}}).

{{Not to be confused with|May 29|qq=May 29}}<!--why is this category always showing up as 347 members?-->
{{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=五月廿九|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 5월 29일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar May|qq=}} '''May 29''' is the 29th day of the month {{ill|May (Lunisolar)|ja|5月 (旧暦)|lt=May (Satsuki)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May (Lunisolar)|qq=}}, the 5th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}. If the month is {{Nihongo|Small|小|qq=}} then this is the last day of the month. Otherwise, if the month is {{Nihongo|Big|大|qq=}}, then it is the second last day.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref> Its {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuyō|qq=}} day is {{ill|Senbu|WD=Q96759842|1=senpu}}{{efn|ja|先負|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}{{efn|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 六曜|1=Rokuyō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9418169}}.
== Events == * {{ill|Bunkyū|en|Bunkyū|WD=Q730266|lt=Bunkyū|1=Bunkyū}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1862|en|1862|WD=Q7698|lt=1862|1=1862}} {{ill|June 26|en|June 26|WD=Q2661|lt=June 26|1=June 26}}) – In the 【Second Tōzenji Incident|{{ill|Tōzenji Incident|ja|東禅寺事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzenji Incident|1=Tozenji incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11103924}}】, a samurai from the Matsumoto domain who was assigned to guard the provisional British legation at Tōzenji in Edo invaded and killed two British seamen.<ref>{{Cite book |和書 |author=池田正一郎 |title=日本災変通志 |publisher=新人物往来社 |year=2004-12-15 |page=721 |isbn=4-404-03190-4|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Meiji 4|Meiji 4]] ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}}) – A large fire broke out in Yoshiwara, with two-thirds of the red-light district burning down; 678 households were destroyed. * Meiji 5th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|WD=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872|1=1872}} {{ill|July 4|en|July 4|WD=Q2698|lt=July 4|1=July 4}}) – Tokyo Normal School (later Tokyo Education University, now part of Tsukuba University) was established.

Shunsai), a Confucian scholar and the son of Hayashi Razan (died {{ill|1680|en|1680|WD=Q7642|lt=1680|1=1680}}). * {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|WD=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1819|en|1819|qid=Q6967|lt=1819|1=1819}} {{ill|July 20|en|July 20|qid=Q2728|lt=July 20|1=July 20}}) – {{ill|Tokugawa Nariharu|en|Tokugawa Nariharu|qid=Q3336095|lt=Tokugawa Saion|1=Tokugawa Nariharu}}, the 11th daimyō of the Owari Domain (died {{ill|1839|en|1839|qid=Q7616|lt=1839|1=1839}}). * Bunkyū 2nd year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} 1862, June 26) – {{ill|Minasaku Motohachi|ja|箕作元八|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minasaku Motohachi|1=Mitsukuri Genpachi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11603119}}, a historian and the fourth son of Minasaku Akutsubo (died {{ill|1919|en|1919|qid=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}).
== Deaths == * {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|qid=Q1193933|lt=Kenkyū|1=Kenkyū}} 4th year ({{ill|Julian calendar|en|Julian calendar|qid=Q11184|lt=Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar}} {{ill|1193|en|1193|qid=Q19741|lt=1193|1=1193}} {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|qid=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}}) – {{ill|Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja|曾我五郎時致|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soga Jigorō Tokuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曾我時致|1=Soga Tokimune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11515401}}, a samurai and the younger brother of Soga (born {{ill|1174|en|1174|qid=Q19690|lt=1174|1=1174}}). * {{ill|Tenmei|en|Tenmei|qid=Q1194121|lt=Tenmei|1=Tenmei}} 8th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1788|en|1788|qid=Q7765|lt=1788|1=1788}} {{ill|July 2|en|July 2|qid=Q2697|lt=July 2|1=July 2}}) – {{ill|Goryōken Kayū|ja|呉陵軒可有|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Goryōken Kayū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a miscellaneous haiku writer known as "Yanudaru" (柳多留). * {{ill|Bunka|en|Bunka|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 14th year ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}} {{ill|1817|en|1817|qid=Q6958|lt=1817|1=1817}} {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|qid=Q2687|lt=July 13|1=July 13}}) – {{ill|Kaiho Seiryō|ja|海保青陵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaiho Seiryō|1=Kaiho Seiryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6561857}}, a Confucian scholar and economic reformer (born {{ill|1755|en|1755|qid=Q7627|lt=1755|1=1755}}).
